items.headline: Nine new homes approved for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: City Council approved a plan to subdivide a vacant lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This is a significant residential infill project that will add housing to an existing neighborhood.

items.headline: City Council approves lease revenue bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: Mountain View is financing a new public safety building through lease revenue bonds, a major capital project that will affect city services and taxpayer costs. The council and financing authority board approved the funding mechanism on April 28.

items.headline: Mountain View's biodiversity and urban forest plan goes to council review
items.summary: The city is developing a new Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan to guide environmental and green space decisions. Council held a study session on April 28 to review the draft before potential adoption.

items.headline: New restaurant Zingsters opening on University Avenue
items.summary: A new eatery called Zingsters is installing signage at 220 University Avenue in downtown Palo Alto's commercial district. This indicates a new business arrival in the downtown core.

items.headline: Sunnyvale plans mixed-use development with 67 homes and retail
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a proposed 4.22-acre project combining 55 townhomes, 12 apartments, and 18,535 square feet of retail space (including the existing Speedy's Tacos). The development would be subdivided into 17 residential lots plus common areas, representing significant infill growth in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered revising business license tax rates through a municipal code amendment. This annual update affects local business operating costs in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City approves temporary giant signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara will allow large supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The signs will be temporary and limited to the event period, adding distinctive visual branding to the area during the international tournament.

items.headline: Firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
items.summary: The City Council approved a three-year labor agreement with the Santa Clara Firefighters union (Local 1171) covering 2026 through 2029. The deal sets compensation and employment terms for the city's fire department.
